name: Creamy Smoked Salmon & Dill Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Fresh dill, chopped OR 1 tablespoon Dried dill
recipeInstructions:
1. Generously butter a 9-1/2-inch diameter deep-dish pie plate.
2. Place one Filo sheet on the work surface (cover remaining sheets with plastic wrap and then with a clean damp towel).
3. Brush the Filo sheet with butter and fold it in half lengthwise. Brush the folded surface with butter.
4. Cut the Filo into halves cross-wise.
5. Place one Filo rectangle, buttered side down, in the prepared pie plate, covering the bottom and letting the pastry overhang by 1/2-inch on one edge.
6. Brush the top of the Filo in the pie plate with butter.
7. Place a second Filo rectangle in the pie plate, covering the bottom and letting the pastry overhang another section of the edge by 1/2-inch; brush with butter.
8. Repeat this process with the remaining 4 Filo sheets, making sure the entire surface of the edge is covered to form the crust.
9. Fold any overhang under to form a crust edge flush with the edge of the pie plate.
10. Brush the crust edges with butter.
11. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
12. Whisk together Egg yolks and mustard in a medium bowl until blended.
13. Beat in Eggs, Half and half, Cream, Smoked salmon, Scallions, and chopped dill (or dried dill).
14. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
15. Pour the mixture into the prepared crust.
16. Bake until the center is set, about 50 minutes.
17. Transfer to a rack.
18. Cool.
19. Garnish with dill sprigs and serve slightly warm or at room temperature.
NOTE: Purchased Filo pastry is used here instead of a regular pie crust for quick and easy assembly. This tart is best when served at room temperature.
